text: Plaintiff's counsel admitted filing briefs in two companion employment cases containing AI-generated fabricated citations. The U.S. District Court for the Middle District of Florida imposed a $7,000 fee sanction and required counsel to deliver ten speaking engagements at bar associations or law-school groups by January 2027 to educate the profession on AI risks. The court declined to refer counsel to the Florida Bar or the court's Grievance Committee.
canonical_source_conflicted: Non-existent federal case citations filed in two employment-law matters; Fed. R. Civ. P. 11
mitigation_gap: Counsel admitted reliance on AI-generated research without verifying citation existence before filing in both cases.
reliance_or_harm: $7,000 fee sanction plus mandatory 10 bar-association/law-school speaking engagements by January 2027.
